protocol.name: nixbench_application_menu_v1

interface.name: nixbench_application_menu_manager_v1

description.summary: create a global menu for an application surface

interface.description: This opt-in interface lets an application publish the bounded menu model shown by NixBench while its toplevel is focused. It augments standard xdg-shell; it does not provide a widget toolkit or a surface role.

entry.name: foreign_surface
entry.summary: the wl_surface is not owned by this client

entry.name: invalid_role
entry.summary: the wl_surface is not an xdg_toplevel

entry.name: already_exists
entry.summary: the wl_surface already has an application menu

request.name: get_menu

description.summary: associate a menu with an xdg-toplevel surface

request.description: The wl_surface must already have a live xdg_toplevel role and must belong to the requesting client. Only one application-menu object may exist for a surface at a time.

interface.name: nixbench_application_menu_v1
interface.description:
A newly created object starts an empty transaction. append_menu starts a menu and append_item or append_separator adds to the most recently appended menu. commit atomically replaces the surface's published menu. reset starts another empty transaction. A transaction must contain at least one menu before it can be committed.
Menu and item counts, string lengths, and command identifiers are bounded by the compositor. Commands are nonzero and unique across one committed model. Destroying this object restores the compositor's fallback menu for the surface. Destroying the associated xdg_toplevel makes this object defunct; a replacement toplevel needs a new menu object.

entry.name: defunct_surface
entry.summary: the associated surface no longer exists

entry.name: invalid_state
entry.summary: request is not valid in the current transaction

entry.name: invalid_label
entry.summary: a label is empty or exceeds the compositor limit

entry.name: limit_exceeded
entry.summary: the compositor's bounded menu capacity was exceeded

entry.name: invalid_command
entry.summary: a command is zero or duplicated

entry.name: enabled
entry.summary: the command can currently be activated

entry.name: checked
entry.summary: draw a check mark beside the command

description.summary: destroy the menu and restore the fallback
discard the pending transaction and start over

request.name: append_menu
request.description: Subsequent items and separators are appended to this menu until the next append_menu request.

description.summary: append a command to the current menu
append a separator to the current menu
atomically publish the pending menu model

event.name: command
event.description: The command is one of the enabled identifiers from the most recently committed model.
